name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-17
title: Concurrent invocations race on worktree path causing FileNotFoundError crashes
description: branch_ops._worktree uses a single fixed path .claude/tmp/bug-backlog-files. When two file-item.py / item-status.py invocations run concurrently (parallel agents, parallel CI jobs), one shutil.rmtree(wt) races with the other git operations / write_text, producing FileNotFoundError mid-execution. Observed live during housekeeping: multiple cascading failures (no such file or directory: item.json) and non-fast-forward push rejections. Allocated IDs were burned with no item.json written. Fix: use a per-process worktree path (include PID or UUID) so concurrent invocations do not clobber each other. The always cleans up invariant is correct for sequential use but unsafe for any parallelism.

history.note: Wave 4 triage: duplicate of RABBIT-FILE-BUG-18 (concurrent worktree race), fixed by PR #112
